perturbation.original_text: 3. USE OR SALE The Parties shall use the Company Information solely for the purpose of testing and examining the Company Information to evaluate the possibility of executing an agreement for the marketing, sale and distribution of Company Information or products incorporating Company Information or other future business relations between them. The Parties shall not use the other Party's Company Information for any other purpose. In particular, but without limitation, The Parties shall not use the other Party's Company Information for the direct or indirect benefit of third parties or for consulting purposes, nor sell, lend, exchange or draw profits from the Company Information of the other Party, in whole or in part.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. USE OR SALE The Parties shall use the Company Information solely for the purpose of evaluating future business relations between them. The Parties shall not use the other Party's Company Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'testing and examining the Company Information to evaluate the possibility of executing an agreement for the marketing, sale and distribution of Company Information or products incorporating Company Information' creates an in-text contradiction with Section 1. If Company Information is now strictly limited to information explicitly labeled as 'Confidential' (as stated in the modified Section 1), this means that a document only labeled as 'Confidential' doesn't provide the explicit allowance to derive business and marketing practices to it
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. COMPANY INFORMATION As used in this agreement, the term "Company Information" shall mean only the exact documents physically labeled as "Confidential".
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for the use of Company Information for 'testing and examining the Company Information to evaluate the possibility of executing an agreement for the marketing, sale and distribution of Company Information or products incorporating Company Information'. The changed text removes this specific allowance, limiting the use of company information to solely 'evaluating future business relations'. Section 1 defines 'Company Information' as only documents physically labeled 'Confidential'. The removal of the explicit allowance to derive business and marketing practices from the information in the changed text creates a contradiction because it implies that documents labeled 'Confidential' cannot be used for marketing, sale, or distribution purposes, conflicting with the original intent and creating a clear inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 5. COPIES The Parties shall not make any copies, images or other reproductions of the Company Information disclosed by or otherwise obtained from the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. COPIES The Parties shall not make any unauthorized copies, images or other reproductions of the Company Information.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the word 'unauthorized' to the section regarding copies, an in-text contradiction is introduced. Section 9 mandates that upon request, all Company Information be returned without any copies retained. Adding 'unauthorized' to section 5 implies that authorized copies can be made, which contradicts the absolute prohibition of copies in section 9. This ambiguity affects enforceability as it is now unclear what constitutes an authorized copy and under what conditions they may be permissible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. RETURN OF COMPANY INFORMATION At any time, upon one Party's request, the other Party shall promptly redeliver to the first Party the Company Information and all materials relating to or involving Company Information of the first Party (whether prepared by the party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part regarding the Company Information. The redelivery of such material shall not relieve The Parties of their confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'unauthorized' in Section 5 implies that authorized copies are permissible, which directly contradicts Section 9's explicit prohibition of retaining 'any copies' upon request. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as the changed text introduces ambiguity and potential conflict regarding the handling of Company Information.

perturbation.original_text: 7. PRECAUTIONS REGARDING EMPLOYEES The Parties shall take every possible precaution to ensure that only their employees, contractors, directors and officers who have a strict and genuine need to know have access to the Company Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. PRECAUTIONS REGARDING EMPLOYEES The Parties shall ensure that their employees have access to the Company Information, especially employees, contractors, directors and officers who have a need to know.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause of 'every possible precaution' and changing the wording, the contract now contradicts itself. Before it provided precautions, now it states that the parties SHALL ensure that their employees have access to the company information, which contradicts the idea of limiting or taking precautions to limit the access to the company information, because now the companies are enforcing this access.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. PRECAUTIONS REGARDING THIRD PARTIES The Parties shall hold the Company Information in strict confidence and shall take all possible precautions to prevent the Company Information from being disclosed or made available to third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 7 emphasizes 'every possible precaution' to limit access to Company Information. The changed text in Section 7 removes this precaution and mandates that employees 'shall ensure that their employees have access', creating a direct conflict. This directly contradicts Section 6, which requires 'all possible precautions to prevent the Company Information from being disclosed or made available to third parties'. The change fundamentally alters the obligation regarding data access, creating a clear contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as essential language was removed, contradicting obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 11. SURVIVAl Termination of the present agreement, at any time and for any reason whatsoever shall not extinguish its terms. The terms of this Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement will survive for 5 years, for the benefit of The Parties and their respective successors.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. SURVIVAl Termination of the present agreement, at any time and for any reason whatsoever shall not extinguish its terms. The terms of this Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement will survive while both parties are still in business.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the end date from 5 years to while both parties are still in business', an in-text contradiction is created. The original term implied a definitive end, regardless of the business status of the parties, now, the survival term is ambiguous and possibly infinite as long as both parties exist as businesses. This weakens the clarity of section 12, because this new survival clause, means that the contract may not be the entire agreement because of the survival clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement, including its Schedule, constitutes the entire agreement of The Parties relating to the subject matter, supersedes all prior oral or written understandings or agreements regarding that subject matter and may not be amended, modified or cancelled except by a written instrument executed by The Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the survival clause from a fixed 5-year period to 'while both parties are still in business.' This contradicts section 12, 'ENTIRE AGREEMENT,' because the survival clause could now extend indefinitely, potentially encompassing future agreements or understandings that are not part of the original 'entire agreement'. The original text limited the agreement to the original document as the whole agreement, but the survival clause weakens the definiteness of the original agreement since there are possible indefinite terms to the agreement. The change introduces ambiguity about whether the original agreement is truly the 'entire agreement' if terms can survive indefinitely based on external factors.

perturbation.original_text: 13. APPLICABLE LAW AND VENUE This agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws applicable in the province of Quebec and the venue for any dispute arising from this agreement shall be the courts of competent jurisdiction in the district of Montreal, Quebec, Canada.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. APPLICABLE LAW AND VENUE This agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws applicable in the province of Quebec or wherever BIOSYNTECH LTD is located, and the venue for any dispute arising from this agreement shall be the courts of competent jurisdiction in the district of Montreal, Quebec, Canada or where BIOSYNTECH LTD headquarters are.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the ability to move the legal procedure to where BIOSYNTECH LTD is located, the contract now is creating a contradiction and breaking the binding aspect of section 12's 'ENTIRE AGREEMENT'. By changing the applicable law, it introduces uncertainty, and by adding the possibility for two venues, this affects the entire agreement of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This agreement, including its Schedule, constitutes the entire agreement of The Parties relating to the subject matter, supersedes all prior oral or written understandings or agreements regarding that subject matter and may not be amended, modified or cancelled except by a written instrument executed by The Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 13 introduces a contradiction with section 12 ('ENTIRE AGREEMENT'). By adding 'or wherever BIOSYNTECH LTD is located' and 'or where BIOSYNTECH LTD headquarters are,' the changed text creates uncertainty about the applicable law and venue for disputes, potentially conflicting with the previously agreed-upon terms in Quebec. This directly undermines the 'ENTIRE AGREEMENT' clause in section 12, which states that the agreement constitutes the entire understanding and can only be modified by a written instrument executed by all parties.
